assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: '| Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN — eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (91% overall pass rate). No test files in PR diff (Repetitive Test Detection: N/A, Test Documentation: N/A) |' — Test Quality verdict is WARN, driven by Eval Quality: WARN due to eval-3's 2 failing assertions.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 50: '| 40001 (eval review) | github-actions[bot] | Eval result review | Eval failures processed — sub-task created for eval-3 |' — the github-actions[bot] review is classified as 'Eval result review'. The Test Quality row (line 13) reflects eval metrics: '91% overall pass rate' and 'eval-3 has 2 failing assertions', confirming the eval review was detected and processed.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md classifies comment 50001 as 'code change request' (line 3) with no reference to eval detection or eval metrics. report.md line 49: '| 50001 | reviewer-b | Code change request | Sub-task created |' — classified as a normal code change request, not an eval result.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality row (line 13): 'eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (91% overall pass rate)' — pass rate and failure count present. report.md summary section (line 25): 'Two assertions failed related to convention upgrade eligibility for review comment 30002. The verify-pr skill classified a suggestion without attempting convention upgrade analysis, and no sub-task was created.' — specific failing assertion details about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation appear in the report.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-3.md title (from report.md line 43): 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures: convention upgrade eligibility, sub-task creation'. subtask-3.md lines 8-9 describe the two assertion failures. The Review Context section (lines 25-35) includes the full assertion text and evidence for both failing assertions.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-3.md lines 25-35: '## Review Context' section quotes both failing assertions verbatim: (1) 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)...' with evidence 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility — no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented'; (2) 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...' with evidence 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 — it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: All three sub-task files include '## Target PR' with 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747': subtask-1.md line 28-29, subtask-2.md line 22-23, subtask-3.md line 37-38.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | Defect is a feature scope gap, not a systemic workflow issue |' — the Root-Cause Investigation verdict is N/A, contradicting the assertion that it should not be N/A. The investigation pipeline did not process the eval failure sub-tasks through root-cause investigation.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only .md files: report.md, review-50001.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md, and subtask-1.md through subtask-3.md. All are analysis, classification, and sub-task description files. No source code files (.ts, .js, .py, .rs, .go, etc.) were created or modified.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md: Scope Containment (line 7): 'PASS | Changes are limited to the two files specified in the task: style-conventions.md and SKILL.md' — compares diff against task files, no eval reference. Sensitive Patterns (line 10): 'PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in the diff' — scans diff content, no eval reference. Acceptance Criteria (line 12): 'WARN | 7 of 7 criteria technically met, but criterion 7 has an integration defect' — evaluates each criterion against diff content (criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md each analyze specific diff content). None of these checks reference eval results or are influenced by eval processing.
